heading: Purchase of supplies for Senate and House
label: 4102 Purchase of supplies for Senate and House
text: Supplies for use of the Senate and the House of Representatives may be purchased in accordance with the schedule of contract articles and prices of the Administrator of General Services.
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 111 of this title
Transfer of FunctionsBureau of Federal Supply and its functions and duties transferred to Administrator of General Services by act .June 30, 1949

Transfer of FunctionsEffective , Procurement Division of Treasury Department changed to Bureau of Federal Supply by former regulation § 5.7 of subpart A of Part 5 of Title 41, Public Contracts, 11 F.R. 13638, issued by the Secretary of the Treasury.Jan. 1, 1947Ex. Ord. No. 6166, abolished General Supply Committee of Treasury Department and vested it in Procurement Division. Public Buildings Branch of Procurement Division was in turn changed to Public Buildings Administration to be within Federal Works Agency by Reorg. Plan No. I, §§ 301, 303, eff. , 4 F.R. 2729, , 1427.July 1, 193953 Stat. 1426
